mnemonics: NoSQL = Not Only SQL. Think of it as a flexible toolbox, not just a single screwdriver.
keyPoints: NoSQL databases are designed for unstructured or semi-structured data.
They provide high scalability and flexibility for big data applications.
Common types include document, key-value, column-family, and graph databases.
commonMistakes: Assuming NoSQL means no SQL-like querying capabilities at all.
Using a NoSQL database for highly transactional, ACID-compliant needs where a relational database would be better suited.
